hypothesis: Incorporating a Dirichlet-distributed noise process in place of the standard Gaussian forward diffusion—where the noise schedule is defined over the probability simplex rather than Euclidean space—will inherently satisfy both non-negativity and sum-to-one constraints throughout all diffusion timesteps without requiring explicit regularization terms, thereby achieving higher validity rates and improved coverage of the feasible composition space compared to PCDiff's penalty-based constraint enforcement.
